transcript: We beat consensus on both EPS and revenue this quarter. We are raising full-year guidance on the strength of record backlog. Gross margin is expanding on mix and pricing. Demand across our end markets remains strong demand with order acceleration into Q2.

Results missed consensus on revenue while EPS was in line. We are cutting full-year guidance to reflect soft demand in industrial end markets. Operating margin is contracting on under-absorption. The demand environment remains cautious with customers delaying orders.

Revenue was roughly in line with consensus. We are maintaining full-year guidance. Margins were flat quarter over quarter. End markets are stable but we see both tailwind in aerospace and headwind in autos, a genuinely mixed picture.

A clear beat on EPS and revenue. We are raising guidance for the full year. Margin expanded 120 basis points on operating leverage. Management sees strong demand and record bookings; the pipeline is accelerating.

We missed on EPS as input costs bit. We are cutting guidance for the back half. Gross margin is contracting on freight and materials. Demand is weak and channel inventories remain elevated; we are cautious into the second half.

We beat on revenue and EPS. We are raising the full-year outlook. Margin is expanding as the new fab ramps. AI-driven demand remains strong demand and bookings hit a record; we see acceleration through year end.

Revenue beat consensus modestly while EPS was in line. We are maintaining guidance. Margins were flat. The tone from customers is upbeat but we are watching a possible headwind from tariffs, so a mixed but stable quarter overall.

Results missed across the board. We are withdrawing full-year guidance given the uncertainty. Margin contracted sharply. Demand turned weak mid-quarter and the slowdown accelerated into April; visibility is poor.

Another beat on EPS and revenue. We are raising guidance again. Gross margin is expanding on pricing carryover. Demand is strong demand across all three segments with a record backlog exiting the quarter.

We returned to an in-line quarter. Guidance is maintained. Margins were flat sequentially. End-market tone is neutral: distributors report stable sell-through, neither tailwind nor headwind dominates.
We beat consensus on EPS with revenue in line. We are raising the low end of guidance. Margin is expanding modestly on cost actions. Demand commentary is upbeat with order acceleration in defense.

We missed on revenue while EPS was in line on buybacks. We are cutting the top end of guidance. Margin was flat. Customers turned cautious in July and the funnel shows a slowdown; we expect a soft second half.

A strong beat on both lines. We are raising full-year guidance materially. Gross margin is expanding on the new product mix. Demand is strong demand with record December-quarter bookings already in hand; momentum is accelerating.

We missed consensus on EPS and revenue. We are cutting guidance for Q4. Margin is contracting as pricing rolls off. The demand environment is weak with a broad slowdown in consumer-exposed lines; we are cautious.
We beat on revenue and EPS. Guidance is raised for the full year. Margins are expanding on scale. Management called out strong demand, record win rates, and an accelerating pipeline into 2026.

Results were in line after last quarter's reset. Guidance is maintained. Margin was flat. The tone is cautious but stabilizing: soft demand persists in legacy lines while the new platform shows an early tailwind.

Preliminary results point to a beat on revenue with EPS in line. We are maintaining guidance pending the audit. Margin trend is flat. Commentary is neutral overall with both a tailwind from pricing and a headwind from FX, a mixed close to the year.

We missed on both EPS and revenue. We are cutting the initial 2026 outlook. Margin is contracting on volume deleverage. Demand is weak and order rates show a further slowdown; management is cautious into Q1.
